summarization.a: The normalized NBE signal intensity decreases with increasing plasma exposure time for both Ar plasma and Cl2 plasma.

data_extraction.a:
| Plasma Exposure Time (s) | Normalized NBE Signal Intensity (a.u.) Cl2 plasma| Normalized NBE Signal Intensity (a.u.) Ar plasma|
|---|---|---|
| 0 | 1.0 | 1.0|
| 50 | 0.5 |0.7|
| 100 | 0.45 |0.6|
| 200 | 0.35 |0.5|
| 300 | 0.25 |0.35|
| 350 | 0.25 |0.3|

a.question: Why is it likely that the Ar plasma has less effect than the Cl2 plasma?
a.answer: a	Both plasmas inflict ion bombardment, but only the Cl2 plasma also has a chemical effect on the substrate.

a.question: If the plasma exposure time was extended even more, would there be saturation?
a.answer: a	Especially for the Cl2 plasma curve there seems to be saturating behaviour, so yes.

a.question: What  is the decreae in NBE signal intensity after 350 s of Ar plasma in percentages?
What is the NBE signal intesnity after 240 s of Cl2 plasma?
